item.title: Ask HN: Can AI create demon slayer level animation?
item.description:
I want to create an animation series. I am just a programmer and has little to no idea about DS level animation. Can Anyone tell me how can I learn that level of Animation ? Or is it better to wait sometime, and an AI would be able to create that level animation ?

I am willing to invest 5-6 years by the way.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47873442

Points: 1

# Comments: 0

item.title: I, AI – a memoir written in first person by an AI about its own existence
Ask HN: Where are all the AI disasters?
item.description:
Ok we see AI and agents constantly on HN, but never see people coming clean with there AI disasters. Anyone had disasters or things going wrong?

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47873166

Points: 2

# Comments: 1

item.title: The Ultimate Personal AI Supercomputer – Nvidia DGX Station
Corral: Measuring how LLM-based AI scientists reason, not just what they produce
Ask HN: Is the ongoing AI research driving LLM models to be better?
item.description:
I'm just a curious hobbyist that has ran LLM models locally and follow a lot of content about it. Hope we have a few AI researchers here on HN to clarify this.

When using Opus or Codex vs. a chinese or Open source model, it feels like its reasoning capabilities are basically the same.

The difference is typically in coding. It looks like OpenAI and Anthropic invest a lot in pre-training (paying Mercor and the like).

Also a lot in creating synthetic data, I believe this has bigger AI research involvement and techniques.

Of course, there's the RLHF loop that developers using Anthropic/OpenAI products as well, which provides probably yields very good data.

This ends up creating the perspective that it is smart, after all, it has been trained with what you want to do, so it can do that for you.

But overall, is there really much AI research being done on those companies, or are the AI researchers mostly fine-tuning small aspects of the model, akin to what Google engineers used to do for Google search?

I ask this because this all looks like somebody with money could throw money at the problem and end up with a better model at the end, provided they do what I outlined above better -- with AI research being really not that important.

It still often feels like talking with ChatGPT 4 with just better data.

Even the big upgrade of Claude Code being able to work autonomously looks to be mainly due to it knowing how to grab context and do tool calls (not saying that this is easy), rather than the model's raw performance being better.

Or am I wrong, is there something extremely good on those models that AI researchers discovered that the others don't have? Or is it really mostly Data?

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47872916

Points: 3

# Comments: 2

item.title: Show HN: Vibeyard – An open-source IDE for managing AI coding agents
Hey I made a AI Native personal workspace for the desktop worker
item.description:
I guess I timed it wrong when I shared it before. But I think you might like it. Or maybe it doesn't make sense I don't know anymore.

CorePage turns your homepage into a place where you actually get things done.

Simple direct answers. From any premium AI model. Dozens of one-click apps. 3 color modes. World news. Your AI-native workspace. Try as guest. Experience more by becoming a free user. Go premium to enjoy.

• AI news briefings • Notes → output • Fast, no-fluff AI chat • Built-in tools • Instant search

https://x.com/CorePageAI

https://corepage.ai

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47872758

Points: 1

# Comments: 2
